I’m now a huge fan of Floor Plan, I’ve played remastered and 2, and I’m looking forward to even more floors. There is a perfect amount of challenge and I actually enjoy getting stuck and finally needing the walkthrough to get me past the parts and couldn’t solve. So fun! Keep up the good work.

Many years ago I watched dantdm play the original floor plan and I loved it then when I got a quest I instantly bought floor plan 2 and I loved then I saw there making a remastered I was pumped I get to experience floor plan for myself and I did and man it’s very fun little game just the way the characters act and move the puzzles are making they get you to think but not think that hard It’s a game with good and simple puzzles and I recommend it for somebody’s starter pack if they ever get a quest turbo button if your reading this thank you so much for an amazing game and I really want floor plan 3 but anyway just get the game

I was late to the game for this title. It is a cute little game. Not bad at all.
But short and a bit long in the tooth.
It runs well on the Q3(why they need graphic options on a game like this is beyond me. People just want to play.)
Safe for kids to play as well.
The game is too quiet. I beat the game in 9 minutes, but I'm not asking for a refund, but make the game longer.

Game took me 30 min to complete. The price is cheap but not cheap enough for the very short playtime. Additionally I felt like most of the puzzles were arbitrary and random, favouring just throwing stuff around until it works with little reasoning. I would definitely get floor plan 2 or the IEYTD series instead as those games are great.
Fun

ok the whole game is you are in an elevator, going up and down floors to hunt for 'clues' - except they aren't clues at all, just random crap that you have to move from floor to floor.  So to win the game, grab everything and just keep throwing it through doors until you cant stand it and take the headset off! you now win.
One puzzle with lots of randomness to it -- pick up stuff, throw it at something.  That's all.  I see version 2 is $30.  I wouldn't pay $3.
it's too short

I got this game not expecting much because my friend recommended it to me and he has no taste, but with this game he was right although it’s shorts it also cheap and pretty fun. This game really made me think and it not one of those games where the puzzle suck it’s the complete opposite!
I want a refund because the game does meet my espectations I want my  refund.
The game is okay it was actually really fun but it is also really really short and I have come  upon a lot of crashes which basically ruined the experience
It was way to easy also why is floor plan two cost so much money
Such a great game, I'm hooked!

I didn’t know what to expect when I bought this little gem. But it turned out to be a funny game after figuring out what I had to do. Spent around 1h for finishing the main game and another 15-20mins for the extra part. I’d say it’s totally worth the money and a nice puzzler, not only for beginners.

I really wanted to like it, and I didn't even expect too much, as it was just supposed to be a small, cute puzzle game. In fact, I wouldn't have minded the outdated (although charming) graphics, the clunky interactions, or the lack of dialogue. The problem is that some of the puzzles were just pretty bad.

The puzzles often involve very little logic, sometimes none at all. Because of this, for the most part you are just trying random things to see what happens, which is not very fun. And when they do require some logic, it's usually super basic and straightforward, which isn't very entertaining either.

The game has its charms, as well as a few original things here and there, but for the most part, I don't think it's worth it. There are way better (and much more modern) VR puzzle games out there.
Play this game if you are bored.
Game
Great
Great game, and it's lots of fun, but it is not that long. I bet the game in minutes.

reviews.review_title: Fun, but short. Buy on sale
reviews.review_description: Fun, but short. Buy on sale

I’m definitely dating myself here but this game is basically a fancy VR version of the historically significant phone game app called “100 Floors” or something of that nature. I loved that game, and once tech evolved too much for it to be supported, knock-offs popped up everywhere. But now Floor Plan has brought about an excellent, reimagined version of that game in a much less frustrating way. Millennials rejoice! 🤣

I must comment on the downsides, though: this game is SHORT. Wayyyy too short. To be honest.. I found myself regretting having spent the money for what amounted to maybe an hour of gameplay. I did just buy the sequel, which boasts 2+ hours of play, so hopefully that will be less disappointing—but the up charge in price between the two is absurd for this reason alone.

Also, there is oftentimes not a lot of connectivity between the items you pick up and where they should be used. It should be more intuitive, if that makes any sense. I don’t want to give out any spoilers so I’ll stay as vague as possible but essentially there are many items you pick up that whatever characters or items connected to them aren’t apparently going to want. And thus it becomes a kinda boring process-by-elimination cycle, where you just go from floor to floor clicking on each thing and hoping something will happen.

But the game has still won me over in bringing back a more cartoonish version of 100 Floors!
It simply the best game I’ve played ever
My kids and I loved this game! We hope they make more like it!
